storyInfo: Philae begins a journey of trials, but faces a dilemma when she encounters a village devastated by Originium locusts.

attributes.content: ...You don't need to stay that far away, even if you didn't want my water.
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: Did you come from the desert? Do desert folks not drink water from the forest?
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: N-No, it's not that...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Hey, are you sure you don't need something, food and water? You look like you could collapse at any moment.
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.text: The long journey through the desert has been exhausting.
Head priest, little did I expect that I would face such trials when I decided to set out on my 'journey'.
Physical pain I could endure, but how do I answer questions like what this girl was asking me?

attributes.content: No, I'm fine... you'll get hurt.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: (Shakes head) I hurt the things that I touch...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Why? Are you a poison shaman?
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: No, it's not really poison... just something really troublesome...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Figured. You look more like a priest than a poison shaman. But your horn... you're sick.
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: Sick people need care.
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: Thank you... I just need some rest. I won't disturb you...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Alright... hey, you can come back for the water later if you're thirsty! The bugs haven't gotten to the water here yet!
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: I guess this is the furthest tree from the village.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Let me just pop my tent down here... phew.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Ugh, the path's so barren. I had to make half a ration portion last for days...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I should get some water later. I've been in control this whole way... I wouldn't dry the well up, right?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I wish I was like everyone else...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Maybe I'd be one of those who protect the living, if I didn't know these Arts and didn't have this evil power...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: But why did the River of Life leave me on the side of the living?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Ugh, there were so many things that I couldn't figure out, even when I was at the temple. There are even more of these things now that I'm out here on my own.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I wish I was like everyone else, so that I wouldn't have to worry about this and that... phew...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.text: I wondered all night if I was being too sentimental.
It's not like I never thought about how people might react when I first began my journey. I didn't come out here to make friends, head priest. I came to... find answers.
But my 'journey' doesn't have a clear goal. Is that how things are supposed to go?
Perhaps it was because of thirst, or hunger. Or perhaps it was the stone tablet I was clutching that made me think of the past, of everyone.

That night, like every lonely night on the journey, I mulled over the questions that came to me repeatedly. I put my arms around myself, and gradually fell asleep to the sound of leaves rustling in the night breeze.

attributes.content: The River of Life has been generous this year, head priest. It has been a long flood season.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: This year's harvest will be a good one.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Wait, why does the River of Life continue to rise when it's already so high...?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Head priest! Something's not right with the river...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Forget the ritual! Get the people out of the temple! We need to get to higher ground, go up the hills!
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: You're still here?
Huh?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Did I wake you up? I didn't realize you were sleeping here. I thought you had left.
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: Oh, I saw you last night...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: You remember me? I'm Aisha. Are you traveling? Where are you going?
attributes.name: Savra Girl

attributes.content: ...Uh, I'm Philae.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I... I don't actually know where I'm going.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: How could you not know where you're going? Anyway, I would have warned you if I knew you were gonna spend the night in the forest.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: It's not safe in the forest, especially not for someone who's sick. There are locusts around.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Locusts?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Clack, clack.
Almost on cue, a strange sound came through the forest, rapid and fragmented.
It sounded as though something was carving wood open with sharp teeth. The sound was not particularly loud, but densely packed, like an eerie whisper that lingers in the ear.
Tsk. Those damned things are at it again.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Careful, they bite. Come with me to the village. Who knows when the next wave might come.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: ...Alright, I'll come with you first.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: That sound just now... are those Originium locusts?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.text: I remembered that I noticed the village yesterday because it seemed glimmer in the sunlight. It looked like an oasis in the desert to my dazed eyes.
I looked past Aisha's shoulder at the strange gleam that appeared in the forest.
Grey-white crystals began creeping up the branches, covering up the once-lush leaves. Bulging veins underneath the bark gradually grew, like some kind of lesion.
Several dead Originium locusts lay on the ground, covered in a thin layer of crystalline dust. They struck fear into my heart even without any sign of movement.

attributes.content: You ever seen it before? These bugs have been plaguing the ancestral forest for years.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: They're no ordinary locusts. These evil things chew through steel, water sources, and the hearts of trees!
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: The locusts have been around for a while? Is there a way to drive them out?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: We make pesticides in the village. I was spreading them in the forests just now.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: We used to be able to kill plenty of bugs with it, but it doesn't work so well now...
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Those things aren't afraid of fire. You can't chase them away, and they don't care if you're crying or praying.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Besides, we can't use fire in the ancestral forest... these trees are holy to our village, and we can't afford to accidentally burn them.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Ah, looks like the sunlight's getting brighter. I forgot to tell you last night, but it's not safe in the forest during the day. The village's this way. Keep up with me.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: ...The caravans all say that the ancestral forest looks like an emerald from a distance!
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Besides, the ancestral forest used to be MUCH bigger than it is now! People wouldn't let their kids go into the forest on their own, because it's really easy to get lost.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Hehe, I was always a careless kid. The adults wouldn't let me out of their sight when I went into the forest with them, lest I do something to offend the ancestors and gods.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: The ancestral forest watches over us, granting us food and water. We would not survive without its blessing...
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: But we hardly go into the forest anymore. There are Originium locusts about during the day, so we only go out at night.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Oh, but this path's safe, don't worry! I've walked it many times, and never encountered Originium locusts during the day.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: (Whisper) Almost all the trees along the path are crystallized. The locusts got them... even the grass has wilted.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: This area has been abandoned for a long time. It used to be stockbeast pastures, but the rocks that appeared out of the ground make it impossible for grass to grow.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: People were forced to sell or kill their stockbeasts. I remember how the children cried for days...
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: How could this be... this place has been affected by Originium for so long...?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.text: I looked down sadly at the lifeless twigs on the ground.

attributes.content: Poor things... they were choked to death by Originium crystals.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: (Whisper) All things living should be allowed to live quietly under the sun, like how flowers bloom, how fruits become ripe, and how animals grow big... the Originium consumes it all.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: They didn't even have the chance to refuse.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: You're more sentimental than I thought. Are all priests like that?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: I can see where you're coming from, though. It's the same for people in the village. It's not food that we're most worried about.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: It's how the poor forest is being destroyed, and the living things that had been with us for so long are taken away, without even a chance to protect themselves.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: (...If it was the power of healing that I had... could I restore the crystallized parts?)
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: (Or at least, alleviate the trees' pain.)
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Why are you so far behind me? Keep up, we're almost there.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: The crystallized trees... I'm not familiar with Originium locusts, but I've seen those crystals a lot.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: The trees that we passed are dangerous. If you continue to live in this environment...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Hey, what's going on? Why is everyone outside?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: The damage to the forest just keeps getting worse. We may not have enough food to last the year.
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: And we're running out of things to trade with the caravans...
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: I told you we should have increased the dosage of the pesticide! The amount we're using doesn't do anything!
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: It's a poison. Too much of it could harm the trees. The trees at the grove that I used to pick berries from have all died.
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: It was the locusts that did it, not the pesticide!
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: I don't even dare go near the berry trees these days. A couple of days ago I saw a nest of beastlings lying there. They weren't moving... my kid used to draw their pictures...
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: Friends... perhaps it's really time for us to leave.
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: The locusts have been getting worse each year. They'll devour the whole ancestral forest at this rate.
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: We tried asking nearby villages for help, but the locusts are so many and so fierce, attacking the people who came to help... no one wants to come anymore.
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: Wait, why are we talking about moving away?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Aisha! Wait—
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: And just abandon the ancestral forest? I'm not leaving. You want to leave, you can do it on your own.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: I'll look for casters from other villages, other towns. There must be a way to kill those bugs and heal the sick trees!
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Aisha? No... it's not so simple...
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: I've been out spreading pesticide since morning, going through the whole forest... there are still many healthy trees left!
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: I don't want our traditions to end with me either... but we need to find a way to survive.
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: Do you know how far the nearest water source is? Maybe we could survive the journey, but the children won't.
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: We stayed here because the ancestral forest is here, because our water source is here. How do we survive if we leave? Our lives depend on the forest.
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: If only there was another way...
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.text: —I was suddenly reminded of the River of Life.
I could see it even without closing my eyes.
As the villagers argued, the forest and the village became the temple, the river banks, the farmland, the source of life that I defended and revered.
We stood on top of the hill, gazing down at the wall of murky water surging through. It made the temple seem so small, so fragile.
I thought the wind-worn stones would survive the century under the scorching sun.
I never thought the flood would take them so easily, like cube sugars melting in coffee.
Now I stand before another village about to be swallowed by fate, listening to arguments so familiar they might as well have been from another dream.
I told myself to stay still. Don't interfere. There was nothing I could do to heal their ills.
But––

attributes.content: Traditions don't mean anything if there's nobody left to protect them.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I know a passing traveler like me shouldn't interfere with your village's matters.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: But those crystals are very dangerous... they're going to spread, and it'll be too late by the time they start appearing near the houses!
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Clack, clack.
I know you don't want to abandon your faith. But the forest bestowed its blessings so that you could live.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: If you want... I could protect you on your way to finding another water source—
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: What are you saying, Philae? Why are you on the mayor's side?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: This forest was an oasis that the gods promised us. Abandoning it means going back on our oath... that's what truly brings disaster!
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Clack, clack.
No need to be hasty, Aisha. We're still discussing this.
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: You guys kept telling me to remember the legends of the forest and its blessings. It's you who's forgotten them, isn't it?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Wait, that's...!
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: Clack, clack.

attributes.text: Perhaps I had let my awareness as a guardian slip, and allowed myself to wallow in sentimentality and hesitation.
That was why I failed to notice that sound, coming in every direction, almost like the echoes of leaves rustling in the wind.
It was an adult locust. Its abdomen was grey-white in color, and its wings were filled with cracks. It flew screeching into the village, like the dagger of the god of death.
It was only inches away from Aisha by the time I heard the hungry buzzing of its wings—

attributes.content: What the...?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Ugh!
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Got there in time... are you alright, Aisha? Looks like a small scratch. I have some herbs here...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Huh...?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Look! That locust on the ground...
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: Did it... shrivel up?
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: I saw patterns appear on the ground when it hit the traveler's stone tablet... is that Arts?
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: Is this the caster you found, Aisha? Are we saved?
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: I-I'm sorry, I lost control for an instant... I'm sorry...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I didn't mean to...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: What are you apologizing for? You knew a way to destroy Originium locusts? Why didn't you tell us?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.text: My mouth was open but I didn't know what to say, so all I could do was stare at Aisha, who was staring at the dead Originium locust on the ground, her eyes filled with surprise and joy.

attributes.content: Wow... you shriveled up a huge bug like that with one hit! That was amazing, Philae!
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: It's not like that...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Everybody okay?! Get inside! The locusts are on the move!
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: You come too, traveler.
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: ...So you see, it's not a good power.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: But you saved me and took out that locust!
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: It's the power of death and destruction! It can't heal the village!
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Isn't killing the locusts just like healing the forest?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: ...If I missed by just a little bit, it might have been you who withered, instead of the locust.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: But you managed to control it, didn't you?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: ...Traveler, you're the only sign of hope that we've seen in the last few years.
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: Your Arts just saved someone. We saw it with our own eyes.
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: I know it's a lot to ask, but we're desperate for any solution. If you could just try... maybe it'll change something...
attributes.name: Mayor

attributes.content: I don't know... please, give me some time to think.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: *pant* *pant*... no one saw me sneak out, right?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: This is... the tree where I took shelter last night. Still no crystals... looks like you survived the Originium locusts for another day.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: *sigh* I just don't know what to think... ended up sneaking out when the others split up to find food.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: How could I possibly say yes? They'll be even sadder if something happens to the forest, especially Aisha who loves it so much.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I've never used Arts on such a scale, not to mention that I have to be precise and not harm any other living things... ahh, even the thought makes it nervous! I can't do it!
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: No, I can't just run away quietly. I have to go back and explain to the others that... I just can't do it...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: But... what would happen to the village if I left without helping them? Today it was a few locusts. What happens tomorrow?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: What do I do, head priest...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Over here! I found her!
What...?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: She's here! I think she's trying to run away!
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: Run away? No, I...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: You can't go! If you leave now, my child... you can destroy those locusts! You've already done it once!
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: Why do you give us hope, if you're not here to help us?
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: If you really make everything you touch 'wither', why was Aisha unharmed when you picked her up after she fell? I saw it with my own eyes!
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: I didn't! I can't...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I already explained it...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Uh, why do you keep talking like your ability is some kind of curse? Maybe I should put it another way... can you make that grass wither?
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: I can't do this to an innocent living thing just to prove something!
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: If you left now, how would you be different from the other casters who just abandoned us without even trying? Please, don't go...
attributes.name: Worried Villager

attributes.content: No! D-Don't touch me!
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Is helping us really so hard? We can talk about payment if that's the issue!
attributes.name: Impatient Villager

attributes.content: No...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Hey! What are you guys doing? Don't press her like this!
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: I'm sorry, Aisha, but I can't try it on your ancestral forest... I'm scared that it would make things worse for the village...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I don't understand, Philae... Why do you always take a step back?
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Huh...?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: You wouldn't take my water and kept your distance from me when walking. You said you weren't a poison shaman and said you bring destruction.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Life is not as fragile as you think it is, Philae.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Did I... disappoint everyone again?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Is there a meaning for everyone's existence, head priest? What's the meaning of mine, then?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: You said I would find my path. But at the end of the day, my powers can only be used to 'take away life', not 'give it'.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: The nature of my powers cannot be changed, no matter how much I try to control myself and my Arts.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: The trials that you gave me always led to death... so I learned to control, suppress, stay away.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: But what then?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I couldn't let it go, but what could I do...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Poor trees. It must hurt to have crystals growing all over you.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Everyone's heart must have hurt when Aisha stopped the mayor, when the villagers struggled, and when the mayor talked about moving away.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: What were you thinking when we looked down at the River of Life, head priest?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I don't want my Arts to take any life... it's the power of destruction, and that which is born in nature should not be ended by this human force...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Head priest, I've never thought of any creature as being inherently evil. Even these locusts might have been innocent victims of the influence of Originium.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: But I see and feel even greater pain, and endless destruction.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: This land and this forest deserve to live their lives and futures to the fullest.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: But the locusts are like a plague, spreading uncontrollably. The pain they brought far exceeds the need for their own survival. Is destruction the sole reason for their existence?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: And what about me, head priest? For what purpose do my past, present and future exist?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: ...No, I must not cry.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: The locusts are spreading too fast. If I hesitate any longer, the village, the forest, everything here... these innocent lives have already suffered too much.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: The mayor was right. They won't need to live in fear anymore if they move away. There'll be a way, as long as life goes on. There will be new hope in their new home.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Besides... it wouldn't be the mayor's fault that the village's tradition ended, if I was the one who did it.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I can't allow the crystals to continue spreading. I'll take responsibility and help them find a new water source...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Is this the meaning of my existence, head priest?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: No time to think. The sun's about to rise.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.text: So I put my hands on the ground and breathed out.
It was quiet all around, but I could feel something gathering in the forest, something struggling violently deep below the surface.
A thin mist began to rise from the ground. It was not cold air, but a curious silence, as time seemed to be suppressed on an invisible waterline by my will.
The crystals began to shatter.

Cracks began to appear on the trees on the outside, spreading from the trunks to the branches. As the last bit of lifeforce was wiped away, the Originium crystals lost their support and fell to the ground.
I heard more sounds of shattering coming from the distance. Head priest, this time I didn't close my eyes, like I did in the previous trials.
I watched as the crystallized trees began to wilt, no longer forced to keep themselves upright; I watched locusts fly out and wither in mid-air, dropping to the ground like falling snow.
Tears streamed from my eyes, rolling silently down towards the ground shaking beneath my hands, into this funeral of my making with no other witness.
Only when there was no other sound in the forest did I pull my hands back. The marks of casting were still there upon them.
Do you know, head priest? I saw them clearly for the first time.

attributes.content: The forest is quiet... no sound of wings buzzing, or Originium crystals growing.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: It's over.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: May you rest in peace, free from the control of Originium, never again to become an instrument of destruction. May you be free.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: I'm sorry, blessed trees. All I can do is lay my hand on you... you're free now, never again to feel pain.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Phew... I'll wait a little longer, then go back to the village and admit what I did...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Huh?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Rustle, rustle.
...The sound of leaves rustling in the wind?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: What's going on... some trees are still alive here... and over there...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Don't my Arts cause everything to wither?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: How could this be... the destruction that I brought didn't touch every living thing?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: How could this be... are you real?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Life is not as fragile as you think it is, Philae.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Shouldn't I help with the rebuilding of the temple?
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Hah, worried that we wouldn't do a good job?
attributes.name: Head Priest

attributes.content: N-No, that's not what I mean...
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Life is not as fragile as you think it is, Philae.
attributes.name: Head Priest

attributes.content: The River of Life may have inundated the farms on the banks. But the flood will pass, and the sediment it leaves behind will become fertile soil and nurture new life.
attributes.name: Head Priest

attributes.content: The cycle of life goes on.
attributes.name: Head Priest

attributes.content: But that's because it's the River of Life. It always brings new life.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: So can you, Philae. You can bring life too. Otherwise, why are we standing here, alive and breathing?
attributes.name: Head Priest

attributes.content: Trust yourself, and trust life. It's not as fragile as you think it is.
attributes.name: Head Priest

attributes.text: Only then did I remember what you said to me before I left, head priest.
I couldn't help but reach out to the trees that still lived. I tried to pull my hand back as I realized what I was doing, when a butterfly landed on my fingertip.
The sun began to rise, casting its light upon the forest, and it was exactly as Aisha said it was.
The butterfly glowed blue like a jewel. It shook its feelers a couple of times, then flew away without withering.
Do you know, head priest? My fingertip... itched a little. I discovered that the feeling of being near a living thing was itchy.

attributes.content: Well, it's time to leave. The weather is good. That's a good start.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: This will be far enough, Aisha.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Are you sure you just want some writing tools and paper? I made these rations. Take it with you.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: I'll be fine, I have enough on me.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: Besides, I never cleared up the misunderstanding with the other villagers... I never did any 'cleansing'. I can't accept gifts that I don't deserve.
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.content: You priests are so humble... don't forget to write to me, then! And tell me when you figure out where you're going.
attributes.name: Aisha

attributes.content: Yes, I will. Before that, though, I need to write home. Thanks to you guys, I've made discoveries that I can't wait to share—
attributes.name: Philae

attributes.text: I apologize, head priest, but I need to say this again—I'm going on a journey again. Farewell.

There are still a lot of things that I don't understand, but I think I've figured out why I'm setting out on this journey, what I'm looking for, and what I can do, not just for the answer, but also to come to terms with myself.
I locked my own possibilities away in a little box. I was afraid to touch it, and afraid that it would touch others. That's why I was confused and hesitant.
But the events of the past several days made me realize that it's okay to touch...
